corrections.text: The article states: 'Digital Health News reported that the platform was used by approximately 65% of U.S. physicians across nearly 27 million clinical encounters in April.' This statistic does not appear in the cited Digital Health News source or in any of the nine sourced articles. The claim is unsourced and should be disregarded.

Three source-outlet misattributions appear in the article body: (1) Dr. Shaun Miller's quote ('We opted for an enterprise-wide rollout to ensure equitable access and gather broad feedback across specialties.') is attributed to HIT Consultant but actually appears in PYMNTS. (2) Daniel Nadler's quote ('This agreement is our first to expand access to the entire care team, including all Mount Sinai nurses and pharmacists.') is attributed to HIT Consultant but actually appears in Digital Health News. (3) The 'last mile' problem framing is attributed to HLTH but the language actually appears in HIT Consultant. All quoted facts are accurate and verifiable; only the outlet attributions are incorrect.
